title: Rafah Crossing Reopens for Limited Medical Evacuations
detail: The Rafah border crossing between Gaza and Egypt reopened on March 19–22, 2026, for limited medical evacuations and returns to Gaza. The crossing, closed for extended periods during the conflict, was opened under Egyptian and Qatari mediation as part of Phase One ceasefire commitments. Medical staff and critically injured Palestinians were evacuated. Humanitarian organizations warned that the limited access remained insufficient to address the acute needs of Gaza's 2.3 million population, and that full reconstruction funding — estimated at $50+ billion by the UN — remained almost entirely unpledged.

title: Netanyahu Submits 55-Page Response to Oct. 7 State Comptroller Investigation
detail: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu submitted a 55-page document in February 2026 with his answers to the state comptroller's investigation into failures leading to October 7. Netanyahu's response placed blame on the military and intelligence establishment — specifically Shin Bet Director Ronen Bar (who resigned March 2025) and IDF Chief Halevi (who resigned January 2025). Netanyahu denied personal responsibility for the intelligence failures, arguing he was not given specific, actionable warnings. The Israeli High Court had previously ordered the comptroller to freeze key parts of the investigation in December 2025. The full findings have not been publicly released.
